steps.title: Identify the student's requirement
steps.content: The student needs a sentence that explains whom the A.M. Turing Award is named after. The notes state explicitly: "It is named after groundbreaking British mathematician Alan Turing." Only a choice that directly provides this information fulfills the requirement.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Check each choice against the requirement:
  • Choice A — 'The A.M. Turing Award is given for "major contributions of lasting importance to computing."': Describes what the award is given for, not whom it is named after. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The A.M. Turing Award is named for groundbreaking British mathematician Alan Turing.': Directly states whom the award is named after, matching the student's requirement. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It was in 2006 that Frances E. Allen won the A.M. Turing Award.': States when a recipient won the award; does not address the naming origin. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In 2006, Frances E. Allen won the A.M. Turing Award for seminal contributions to optimizing compiler techniques.': Describes a recipient's accomplishments; does not explain whom the award is named after. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
